LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EDWARD NYQUIST'S MOTION TO RECLASSIFY DEFENDANTS CROSS-COMPLAINT AS A LIMITED JURISDICTION MATTER IS DENIED. THE TEST FOR RECLASSIFICATION OF A COURT'S JURISDICTION FROM UNLIMITED TO LIMITED, BASED ON AN ASSESSMENT THAT DAMAGES RECOVERED WILL NOT EXCEED $25,000, IS WHETHER THE LACK OF UNLIMITED JURISDICTION IS CLEAR OR VIRTUALLY UNATTAINABLE; UNDER THIS STANDARD, A MATTER MAY BE RECLASSIFIED AS A LIMITED CIVIL ACTION WHEN DURING THE COURSE OF PRETRIAL LITIGATION, IT BECOMES CLEAR THAT THE MATTER WILL NECESSARILY RESULT IN A VERDICT BELOW THE UNLIMITED JURISDICTIONAL AMOUNT. ?(SEE YTUARTE V. SUPERIOR COURT (APP. 2 DIST. 2005) 129 CAL.APP.4TH 266.) THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF CHAKA LOCKHART, A TELEGRAPH HILL PROGRAM INITIATIVE, INC. EMPLOYEE WHO OVERSEES THE COMPANY'S FINANCES, THAT THE DAMAGES EXCEED $100,000 AND THE DECLARATION STATEMENTS OF BRIAN WARD, TELEGRAPH HILL PROGRAM INITIATIVE, INC.'S PRESIDENT, REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF ALLEGED TRANSFERS FROM THE COMPANY'S ACCOUNT TO EDWARD NYQUIST'S PERSONAL ACCOUNT INDICATE THAT THERE IS NOT A "LEGAL CERTAINTY" THAT THE DAMAGES IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WILL BE LESS THAN $25,000. (PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBIT I DEPOSITION OF CHAKA LOCKHART PAGE 85; DECLARATION OF BRIAN WARD PARAGRAPH 4.) THUS, THE MOTION FOR RECLASSIFICATION IS DENIED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORMAL FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EDWARD NYQUIST'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF ARMANDO CASTRO AND ROBERT PARKER AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND DEFENSE COUNSEL IS DENIED AND THE SANCTIONS REQUESTED BY BOTH SIDES IS ALSO DENIED. THE MOTION IS NOW MOOT DUE TO MR. NYQUIST'S DISMISSAL OF HIS FIRST FIVE CLAIMS AND THE RULING ON HIS SIXTH CLAIM. EVEN IF THE MOTION WAS NOT MOOT, IT LACKS MERIT BECAUSE THE JOINT CLIENT EXCEPTION CEASED TO APPLY AFTER MR. NYQUIST'S EMPLOYMENT AT THPI WAS TERMINATED. NOTWITHSTANDING THE LACK OF MERIT TO THE MOTION, BECAUSE OF THE NOVELTY OF THE ISSUE RAISED BY THE MOTION, MR. NYQUIST'S POSITION WAS NOT LACKING IN SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. MR. SCHWACHTER IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion Fo Relief From Waiver
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bracamontes of Bracamontes & Vlasak telephone number 415-835-6777 for Petitioner Edward Nyquist.
Joseph Schwachter of Littler Mendelson P.C. telephone number 415-677-3154 for Defendants Telegraph Hill Program Initiatives, Inc. and David Ward.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EDWARD NYQUIST'S Motion Fo Relief From Waiver of his right to invoke the Fifth Amendment as to any discovery for which he has not provided a substantive response is granted. The Fifth Amendment is an extraordinarily important constitutional right that is not and should not be lost by either the failure to invoke it on a timely basis in objections to written discovery or by prior statements not made at trial or in a court hearing that disclose some of the information for which the Fifth Amendment is later invoked. Moreover, the court credits the position of Mr. Nyquist that he didn't fully appreciate the potential criminal law jeopardy he faced until the recent provision of extensive information by THPI to the district attorney and thus finds that the failures by Mr. Nyquist to previously invoke the Fifth Amendment are excusable and that this motion is made in good faith. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Defendant David Brian Wards And Defendant And Cross-Complainant Telegraph Hill Program Initiatives Summary Judgment Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bracamontes of Bracamontes & Vlasak telephone number 415-835-6777 for Petitioner Edward Nyquist.
Joseph Schwachter of Littler Mendelson P.C. telephone number 415-677-3154 for Defendants Telegraph Hill Program Initiatives, Inc. and David Ward.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DAVID WARD and TELEGRAPH HILL PROGRAM INITIATIVES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY adjudication on all claims alleged in Edward Nyquist's complaint is granted. Mr. Nyquist dismissed his first through fifth causes of action, leaving his sixth cause of action for involuntary dissolution as his only remaining claim. Mr. Ward and THPI have satisfied their burden of production that that they are entitled to judgment in their favor on the dissolution claim and Mr. Nyquist has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue on that claim. Specifically, per the reasoning of Bauer v. Bauer (1996) 46 Cal. App. 4th 1106 and Stuparich v. Harbor Furniture Manufacturing (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 1268, Mr. Ward and THPI have shown that Mr. Nyquist is not entitled an order dissolving THPI under Corporations Code 1800(b)(5) because: 1) there was no history of paying dividends by THPI prior to My Nyquist ceasing to be actively involved with THPI; 2) Mr. Nyquist's employment at TPHI and his position as a member of the board of directors of THPI were not terminated due to a "corporate squeeze out" but rather because of claimed improper actions by Mr. Nyquist; 3) Mr. Nyquist is not being treated differently than any other shareholder with respect to the payment of dividends; and 4) Mr. Ward and THPI do not dispute that Mr. Nyquist is a shareholder of THPI or the amount of the shares he holds. (See especially Defendants' Facts 26-27, 50-51 and 61-63). The fact that Mr. Nyquist is not receiving any dividends or other revenue from his ownership interest in THPI is not a permissible ground for him to dissolve THPI, at least till the taint of his asserted bad faith toward THPI is dissipated and/or THPI or Mr. Ward take some unfair or discriminatory action toward him. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Reclassify Defendants Cross-Complaint As A Limited Jurisdiction Matter
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EDWARD NYQUIST'S Motion To Reclassify Defendants Cross-Complaint As A Limited Jurisdiction Matter is denied. The test for reclassification of a court's jurisdiction from unlimited to limited, based on an assessment that damages recovered will not exceed $25,000, is whether the lack of unlimited jurisdiction is clear or virtually unattainable; under this standard, a matter may be reclassified as a limited civil action when during the course of pretrial litigation, it becomes clear that the matter will necessarily result in a verdict below the unlimited jurisdictional amount. ?(See Ytuarte v. Superior Court (App. 2 Dist. 2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 266.) The deposition testimony of Chaka Lockhart, a Telegraph Hill Program Initiative, Inc. employee who oversees the company's finances, that the damages exceed $100,000 and the declaration statements of Brian Ward, Telegraph Hill Program Initiative, Inc.'s president, regarding the amount of alleged transfers from the company's account to Edward Nyquist's personal account indicate that there is not a "legal certainty" that the damages in the cross-complaint will be less than $25,000. (Plaintiff's Exhibit I Deposition of Chaka Lockhart page 85; Declaration of Brian Ward paragraph 4.) Thus, the motion for reclassification is denied. The prevailing party is to provide a formal form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
